title: DotBEP Schema
description: Open format for authoring BIM Execution Plans programmatically.

code.description: Must comply with the naming convention token pattern.

project.description: General metadata about the construction project the BEP belongs to.

id.description: Must comply with the naming convention token pattern.

payload.description: Fields consumed from instance context and passed to the handler.

output.description: Fields the handler must return. Guards on outgoing edges reference these.

key.description: Variable name referenced in effect handlers as config.KEY.

secret.description: If true, the value is masked in the UI after being saved.

trackedAssetTypeId.description: ref AssetType.id — the asset type this workflow operates on.

workflowId.description: ref Workflow.id — spawns child instances of this workflow when the node is entered.

blocking.description: If true, waits for all spawned child instances to complete before accepting outgoing transitions.

responsibleTeamIds.description: ref Team.id[] — if set alongside responsibleRoleIds, actor must satisfy both.

responsibleEmails.description: ref Member.email[] — authorizes specific members regardless of role or team.

triggerEventId.description: ref FlowEvent.id — the event that fires this transition

guard.description: Condition evaluated against the instance context to determine which path to take.

description.description: Short guide description, not it's content

contentPath.description: Relative path to the .md file inside the .bep archive.

idsPath.description: Relative path to the .ids (Information Delivery Specification) file inside the .bep archive.

diff.description: Relative path to inverse diff (RFC 6902). null on v0.0.
Relative path to inverse diff (RFC 6902). null on v0.0.
